memoryArtifacts.headline: Memorial
memoryArtifacts.description:
Joan was the daughter of William de Ferrieres, 5th Earl of Derby and Sibyl Marshal, daughter of William Marshal, 4th Earl of Pembroke and Isabel de Clare.
Joan first married Sir John I de Mohun of Dun …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Ferrers 'the Elder'於大約1233年出生在英國德比夏爾德比時，她的父親Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby42歲，母親Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby33歲。她於1245年之後與John de Mohun結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1267年10月之前在英國素瑞阿丁頓,第一代西德默斯子爵，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦可洛頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Ferrers 'the Elder'于大约1233年出生在英国德比夏爾德比时，她的父亲Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby42岁，母亲Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby33岁。她于1245年之后与John de Mohun结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1267年10月之前在英国素瑞阿丁頓,第一代西德默斯子爵，埋葬在英国英格兰倫敦可洛頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ferrers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: locative name from Ferrières-Saint-Hilaire (Eure) and Ferrières (Manche).

variants.name: Ferris
meanings: Irish and Scottish: shortened Anglicized form of Irish Ó Fearghuis or Ó Fearghasa ‘descendant of Fearghus’, or from the Scottish-Gaelic form of this personal name, Fearghus (see Fergus ).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Ferrières-Saint-Hilaire, Eure and Ferrières, La Manche in Normandy, France.

variants.name: Ferridge
meanings: English: unexplained; possibly a form of Ferris and therefore a variant of Ferrers .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Ferrers 'the Elder' was born about 1233, in Derby, Derbyshire, England, her father, Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby, was 42 and her mother, Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby, was 33. She married John de Mohun after 1245. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died before October 1267, in Addington, Surrey, England, and was buried in Croydon, London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Ferrers 'the Elder' est née environ 1233, à Derby, Derbyshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby, avait 33 ans. Elle a épousé John de Mohun après 1245. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée avant octobre 1267, à Addington, Surrey, Angleterre, et a été enterrée à Croydon, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Ferrers 'the Elder' um 1233 in Derby, Derbyshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby 42 und ihre Mutter Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby 33. Sie hat nach 1245 John de Mohun geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb vor Oktober 1267 in Addington, Surrey, England und wurde in Croydon, London, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Ferrers 'the Elder' è nata il circa 1233 nel Derby, Derbyshire, England, sua padre, Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby, aveva 42 anni e sua madre, Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby, aveva 33 anni. Lei sposato John de Mohun dopo il 1245. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì prima del ottobre 1267, nel Addington, Surrey, England, e fu sepolta a Croydon, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Ferrers 'the Elder'が約1233年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアデルビーで生まれた時，父Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derbyは42歳，母Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derbyは33歳であった。1245年以後にJohn de Mohunと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1267年10月以前にイギリススリーアッドイントンで死去し，イギリスイギリスロンドンクロイドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Ferrers 'the Elder'(이)가 대략 1233년 영국 더비실르 더비에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby(은)는 42세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 1245년 이후 John de Mohun(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1267년 10월 이전 영국 수리 애딩턴에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 크로이던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Ferrers 'the Elder' nasceu aproximadamente 1233, em Derby, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby, tinha 33 anos. Ela casou-se com John de Mohun após 1245. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu antes outubro de 1267, em Addington, Surrey, Inglaterra, e foi sepultada em Croydon, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Ferrers 'the Elder' родилась около 1233 г. в местности дирби, дирбишир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John de Mohun после 1245 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла перед октябрь 1267 г. в местности аддингтон, сурри, Англия и была похоронена в местности кроидон, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Ferrers 'the Elder' nació aproximadamente 1233, en Derby, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Sibyl Marshall , Countess of Derby, tenía 33 años. Se casó con John de Mohun después de 1245. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió antes de octubre de 1267, en Addington, Surrey, Inglaterra, y fue sepultada en Croydon, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
